message.createfirefightingadd.intake_bou: Intake position bound to bucket controller
message.createfirefightingadd.bind_too_f: Too far from bound water intake!
item.createfirefightingadd.pneumatic_ham: A heavy tool powered by _backtank air_. It works as a basic tool normally, then performs _3x3_ breaking or area attacks when charged.
Consumes _100_ units of backtank air and becomes charged.
When left-clicking a block while charged
Breaks mineable blocks in a _3x3_ area around the target.
Strikes the target heavily and deals area damage around it.
item.createfirefightingadd.multipurpose_: A wearable compound backtank that stores both _compressed air_ and _fluid_.
Supplies air like a Create backtank and provides extra protection.
Uses its bottom face as a fluid port. With top kinetic input, it pumps fluid in or out according to its current mode while refilling air.
item.createfirefightingadd.handheld_nozz: Connects to a Fire Hydrant Cabinet and becomes a _handheld nozzle_, rendering a thin hose between the player and cabinet.
Shift + right-clicking a Fire Hydrant Cabinet
Binds to a cabinet containing both a hose and a nozzle.
When holding left click while bound
Consumes fluid supplied by the cabinet and sprays from the handheld nozzle.
block.createfirefightingadd.fire_pole.to: Firefighters always slide _safely_ down fire poles, then rush to the rescue scene.
When sliding down while hugging the pole
Lets you slide down the pole while _preventing fall damage_, though it is _a little slower_ than jumping straight down.
When right-clicking an existing pole with another Fire Pole
createfirefightingadd.handheld_nozzle.mi: A nozzle is required to spray!
createfirefightingadd.pneumatic_hammer.a: The Pneumatic Hammer is already charged
createfirefightingadd.fire_hose_connecto: Cached fire hose endpoint not found
createfirefightingadd.config.nozzleFireC: Probability (0-100%) that lava spray ignites flammable blocks per tick
createfirefightingadd.config.nozzleBuild: Ticks for spray range to build up from 0 to full distance (1-60). Uses ease-in quadratic for natural pressure buildup
createfirefightingadd.config.renderHandh: Render handheld fire hose in first person
Render handheld fire hose in third person
createfirefightingadd.flow_meter.no_pump: No pump detected on the pipe network
createfirefightingadd.ponder.high_pressu: High Pressure Pump range and pressure
High Pressure Pumps scale Create's mechanical pump behaviour.
The amplification multiplier increases the pipe distance reached by pressure.
At the default multiplier of 2x, a 16 block Create range becomes 32 blocks.
The same multiplier also scales pump pressure, allowing higher flow speeds.
Change HighPressurePump.amplificationMultiplier in the server config to tune both values together.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.nozzle_basi: Cone Nozzles spread a rounded stream, while Flat Spray Nozzles make a wider sheet.
Feed a nozzle with fluid through pipes. Creative tanks are used here as compact examples.
Water, milk, potions, lava, flammable fuels, and dragon breath each use their own spray behaviour.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.nozzle_fire: Sprays can push entities along the stream.
Items on Depots and dropped items can be processed by matching Create fan recipes.
Water extinguishes fires and campfires in the visible spray path.
Walls block spray effects, so protected flames behind the glass remain untouched.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.nozzle_flui: Change the stored fluid to change what the nozzle does.
Lava and ignited fuels use heated processing, light fires, and melt snow or ice.
With Create: Dragons Plus installed, dragon breath gains its own particles and processing type.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.water_intak: Water Intakes search nearby infinite water sources and can find water below moving contraptions.
Right-click the intake while holding a Bucket Controller to bind them.
The intake works only while it has both kinetic stress and a redstone signal.
Bound Bucket Controllers are filled first.
Once the bound controller is full, remaining water is pushed into the rear pipe network.
The connected tank receives the water after the controller has been supplied.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.bucket_cont: Bucket Controllers are used for large-area fire suppression, usually supplied by a Water Intake.
Apply redstone to activate the downward spray.
The falling stream covers a wide area below the controller.
Use the side value setting to adjust spray height. Higher settings narrow the cone.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.fire_hose_c: Fire Hoses carry fluid when driven by an external pump, even across moving physical structures.
The hose inherits the pump range: distance from pump to hose plus distance behind the output hose equals the pump's total range. Non-Create pumps are supported, but may have compatibility issues.
Right-click either end with black dye to use a black hose. White dye restores the original color.
Fire Hose Connector modes and reconnection
Fire Hose Connectors have three modes: Idle, Fixed, and Free.
In Idle mode, a redstone pulse highlights the cached partner endpoint. With no cached endpoint, it reports that the cached endpoint is missing and does not connect.
Fixed mode remembers the last connected hose endpoint and tries to restore that saved connection while it is still in range.
If there is no saved endpoint, or the saved endpoint is missing or too far away, Fixed mode will not choose a different endpoint.
Free mode scans nearby space and connects to the nearest available hose endpoint it can find.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.pipeline_tu: Pipeline Turbines read pressure from their own Create pipe network. Here, the source pump provides speed 32, so the pipe pressure is 32.
Speedometer A reads 32 RPM. A turbine's output speed equals the source pump speed.
Stressometer A reads 48 SU. The source pump consumes 128 SU; 128 x 0.75 = 96 SU total output, split evenly between A and B.
The two Fire Hose endpoints are treated as different pipe networks. Fluid can pass through the connected hose, but pipe-network pressure does not cross it.
Turbine C receives 0 pressure, so Speedometer B reads 0 RPM and Stressometer B reads 0 SU.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.fluid_flow_: Monitoring fluid information using the Flow Meter
The Flow Meter displays fluid information from the pipe it is installed in.
When wearing Engineer's Goggles, the player can read the pump speed, pressure, estimated flow rate, and fluid type.
Here, the source pump runs at 16 RPM. The gauge reads pressure 16.0, estimated flow rate 8.0 mB/t, and Water.
createfirefightingadd.ponder.fire_hydran: The cabinet UI accepts a Fire Hose, a nozzle, and an empty bucket.
A cabinet with a hose, a nozzle, and fluid can serve a Handheld Nozzle Controller.
Sneak-right-click the cabinet with an empty controller to bind it.
The bound controller becomes a handheld nozzle, linked back to the cabinet by a hose.
Hold attack to spray using the cabinet's stored fluid and installed nozzle.
